div.p: Message: Invalid argument supplied for foreach()

serStmt.serInfo: Statistics South Africa. Labour Force Survey: March 2005. [dataset]. Version 1.1. Pretoria: Statistics South Africa [producer], 2005. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2011. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/psyf-8s67

verStmt.version: v1.1: Edited, anonymised dataset for public distribution
verStmt.notes: The South African March 2005 LFS dataset was originally released in September 2005 as 2 data files (person and worker). A second version was downloaded from the Statistics South Africa website subsequent to that in August 2011. This version differs from the original release in that all data files now contain "year" and "month" variables.

stdyInfo.abstract:
The LFS is a twice-yearly rotating panel household survey, specifically designed to measure the dynamics of employment and unemployment in South Africa. It measures a variety of issues related to the labour market,including unemployment rates (official and expanded), according to standard definitions of the International Labour Organisation (ILO).
All editions of the LFS have been updated (some more than once) since their release. These version changes are detailed in a document available from DataFirst (in the "external documents" section titled "LFS 2000-2008 Collated Version Notes on the South African LFS").

sumDscr.geogUnit: Province (variable name: "Prov") Stratum (variable name: "Stratum") District Councile (variable name: "DC") PSU (variable name: "PSU") - Note that this variable is not associated with any particular, publicly available codelist, so while the PSU does identify geographically proximate observations, it is not possible to discern those locations from the data.
sumDscr.universe: The LFS sample covers the non-institutional population except for workers' hostels. However, persons living in private dwelling units within institutions are also enumerated. For example, within a school compound, one would enumerate the schoolmaster's house and teachers' accommodation because these are private dwellings. Students living in a dormitory on the school compound would, however, be excluded.

stdyInfo.notes: Household characteristics, household listing, demographics, education, economic activity, work for pay, business ownership, unemployment, employers, main work activity in the past week, wages, salary, employment, migration

dataColl.sampProc:
Statistics South Africa uses a rotating panel methodology for the labour force survey. The rotating panel methodology involves visiting the same dwelling units on a number of occasions (in this instance, five at most). After the panel is established, a proportion of the dwelling units is replaced each round (in this instance, 20%). New dwelling units are added to the sample to replace those that are taken out.
Enumeration Areas (EAs) that had a household count of less than twenty-five were omitted from the census 2001 frame that was used to draw the sample of Primary Sampling Units (PSUs) for the new Master Sample. Other omissions from the Master Sample frame included all institution EAs except workers, hostels, convents and monasteries. EAs from census 2001 were pooled in two stages, before and after sampling. Before sampling the criterion that was used to pool EAs was that they should contain a minimum of one hundred households. However, during listing it was discovered that there were discrepancies between the information on the database and what was on the ground.
Therefore, in the second stage of pooling, EAs that were found to have less than sixty dwelling units during listing were pooled. The Master Sample is a multi-stage stratified sample. The overall sample size of PSUs was 3000. The explicit strata were the 53 district councils/metros (DCs). The 3000 PSUs were allocated to these DCs using the power allocation method. The PSUs were then sampled using probability proportional to size principles. The measure of size used was the number of households in a PSU as calculated in the census. The sampled PSUs were listed with the dwelling unit as the listing unit. From these listings systematic samples of dwelling units per PSU were drawn. These samples of dwelling units form clusters. The size of the clusters differs depending on the specific survey requirements. The LFS uses one of the clusters that contain ten dwelling units.
dataColl.weight: The initial weights (household weights), based on the sample design, were equal to the inverse of the probability of selection. The initial weight for each member of the household was the same as the weight for the household itself. Further adjustment factors were then calculated within PSUs to account for non-response. To adjust for under-enumeration and to align survey estimates with independent population estimates, the weights were calibrated against Person benchmarks. A software package called CALMAR was used to perform this calibration. Using an iterative procedure, CALMAR adjusted the weights so that Person estimates conformed as closely as possible to external Person benchmarks. Gender, race and age group parameters were used for the Person cross-classification of the population.

useStmt.citReq: Statistics South Africa. Labour Force Survey: March 2005. [dataset]. Version 1.1. Pretoria: Statistics South Africa [producer], 2005. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2011. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/psyf-8s67

catgry.labl: Dwelling / House or brick structure on a separate stand or yard or on farm
Traditional dwelling / Hut / Structure made of traditional materials
Flat or apartment in a block of flats
Town / Cluster / Semi-detached house (Simplex, Duplex or Triplex)
Informal dwelling / Shack not in backyard, e.g. in an informal/squatter settlement or on farm
Dwelling / House or brick structure on a separate stand or yard or on farm
Traditional dwelling / Hut / Structure made of traditional materials
Flat or apartment in a block of flats
Town / Cluster / Semi-detached house (Simplex, Duplex or Triplex)
Informal dwelling / Shack not in backyard, e.g. in an informal/squatter settlement or on farm
Owned, but not yet fully paid off (e.g. with a mortgage)
Occupied rent-free as part of employment contract of family member
Occupied rent-free not as part of employment contract of family member
Living together like husband and wife
Diploma with less than Grade 12 / Std 10
Physical, mathematical, computer and life sciences
More than 1 week ? less than 2 weeks
Physical, mathematical, computer and life sciences
Adult basic education and training / literacy classes
Other than any of the above
Diploma / certificate with less than Grade 12 / Std 10
Diploma with less than Grade 12 / Std 10
Physical, mathematical, computer and life sciences

var.name: Q23Rsnab
var.labl: Main reason for absence from activity
var.imputation: Main reason for absence from activity
var.security: Main reason for absence from activity
var.embargo: Main reason for absence from activity
var.respUnit: Main reason for absence from activity

catgry.labl: Caring for family or others (except maternity / paternity leave)
Temporarily laid off / reduction in economic activity
Later than four weeks from now
Has found a job, but is only starting at a definite date in the future
Scholar or student and prefers not to work
Housewife/homemaker and prefers not to work
Retired and prefers not to seek formal work
Illness, invalid, disabled or unable to work (handicapped)
06 = Too young or too old to work
Seasonal worker, e.g. fruit picker, wool-shearer
Lack of skills or qualifications for available jobs
Cannot find suitable work (salary, location of work or conditions not satisfactory)
Contract worker, e.g. mine worker resting according to contract
Later than four weeks from now

var.name: Q38SeekHh
var.labl: Steps taken to look for work
var.imputation: Steps taken to look for work
var.security: Steps taken to look for work
var.embargo: Steps taken to look for work
var.respUnit: Steps taken to look for work

catgry.labl: Enquired at workplaces, farms, factories or called on other possible employers
Sought assistance from relatives or friends
Looked for land, building, equipment or applied for permit to start own business or farming
Waited at the street-side where casual workers are found
Has been temporarily laid off work
Undergoing training to help find work
No jobs available in the area
Lack of money to pay for transport to look for work
Unable to find work requiring his/her skills
Lost hope of finding any kind of work
More than a week but less than 1 month

var.name: Q315inHH
var.labl: Supported by persons in the household
var.imputation: Supported by persons in the household
var.security: Supported by persons in the household
var.embargo: Supported by persons in the household
var.respUnit: Supported by persons in the household

var.name: Q315notH
var.labl: Supported by persons not in household
var.imputation: Supported by persons not in household
var.security: Supported by persons not in household
var.embargo: Supported by persons not in household
var.respUnit: Supported by persons not in household

var.name: Q315Othr
var.labl: Other sources e.g. bursary, study loan
var.imputation: Other sources e.g. bursary, study loan
var.security: Other sources e.g. bursary, study loan
var.embargo: Other sources e.g. bursary, study loan
var.respUnit: Other sources e.g. bursary, study loan

catgry.labl: Working for someone else for pay
Working for one or more private households as a domestic employee, gardener or security guard
Working on his/her own or on a small household farm/plot or collecting natural products from the forest or sea
Working on his/her own or with a partner, in any type of business (including commercial farms)
Helping without pay in a household business
Both the employer and the person him/herself
Tools and/or equipment are rented/hired or owned by an outside person or organisation
The establishment/enterprise/individual for which he/she works

var.name: Q411Pens
var.labl: Contribution to pension or retirement fund
var.imputation: Contribution to pension or retirement fund
var.security: Contribution to pension or retirement fund
var.embargo: Contribution to pension or retirement fund
var.respUnit: Contribution to pension or retirement fund

catgry.labl: A club, community organisation, welfare organisation, NGO or a church
A co-operative, self-help association, labour union, professional association, or business league
A private business or a private household
No, because hi/her income is above UIF limit
Yes, for him/herself and his/her dependants
No, because he/she is covered by someone else?s medical aid fund/ health insurance
In the informal sector (including domestic work)
In the owner's home/On the owner?s farm/
In someone else's home / Private household
Inside a formal business premises such as factory or office
At a service outlet such as a shop, school, post office, etc
On a footpath, street, street corner, open space or field

var.name: Q423bHrs
var.labl: Hours worked past week ? other activities
var.imputation: Hours worked past week ? other activities
var.security: Hours worked past week ? other activities
var.embargo: Hours worked past week ? other activities
var.respUnit: Hours worked past week ? other activities

var.labl: Hours worked past week- in total
var.imputation: Hours worked past week- in total
var.security: Hours worked past week- in total
var.embargo: Hours worked past week- in total
var.respUnit: Hours worked past week- in total

catgry.labl: He/she can decide fully for him/herself
He/she can decide, but within a limited range (e.g. flexitime)
Number of hours are fixed by his/her employer

var.name: Q428look
var.labl: Action to look for extra work
var.imputation: Action to look for extra work
var.security: Action to look for extra work
var.embargo: Action to look for extra work
var.respUnit: Action to look for extra work

var.name: Q429Seekk
var.labl: Activities done to look/prepare for extra wor
var.imputation: Activities done to look/prepare for extra wor
var.security: Activities done to look/prepare for extra wor

var.respUnit: Activities done to look/prepare for extra wor

catgry.labl: Enquired at workplaces, farms, factories or called on other possible employers
Waited/registered at employment agency, labour broker, department of labour or trade union
Sought assistance from friends or relatives
Looked for land, buildings or equipment or applied for permit to start own business or farming
As a main source of food for the household
As the main source of income/earning a living
As an extra source of income
As an extra source of food for the household
As a leisure activity or hobby, e.g. gardening
Financial intermediation, insurance, real estate and business services
Service workers and shop and market sales workers
Plant and machine operators and assemblers
Service workers and shop and market sales workers
Plant and machine operators and assemblers
